steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence’s subject is ‘each one of the thousands of faux Chinese characters.’ The head noun is ‘each one’ — always singular — regardless of the plural ‘thousands’ in the prepositional phrase. The intervening dash-enclosed modifier does not change the subject. We need the singular present-tense verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘hold’: plural present-tense form. The singular subject ‘each one’ requires a singular verb. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘holds’: singular present-tense form. Agrees with the singular subject ‘each one.’ ✓
  • Choice C — ‘have held’: plural present-perfect form. Fails on both number (plural) and introduces an unnecessary perfect aspect. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘were holding’: past-progressive form. Wrong tense and wrong number for this descriptive present-tense sentence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
